passages.title: The History of the Internet
passages.text:
The internet has transformed nearly every aspect of modern life, but its origins are surprisingly modest. The story begins in the 1960s during the Cold War, when the United States Department of Defense sought to create a communication network that could survive a nuclear attack. The result was ARPANET, a network that connected computers at universities and research institutions.

ARPANET sent its first message on October 29, 1969, between UCLA and Stanford Research Institute. The system was designed to be decentralized - if one node was destroyed, data would automatically route around the damage. This principle of distributed networking became the foundation of the modern internet.

Throughout the 1970s, computer scientists developed protocols that would allow different networks to communicate with each other. Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn developed the TCP/IP protocol suite, which became the standard for internet communication in 1983. TCP/IP breaks data into packets, sends them across the network, and reassembles them at the destination.

The 1990s marked the internet's transition from academic to commercial use. Tim Berners-Lee, a British scientist working at CERN, invented the World Wide Web in 1989, introducing HTML, HTTP, and URLs. The first web browser, Mosaic, was released in 1993, making the web accessible to non-technical users. By the end of the decade, millions of people were online.

The 2000s brought Web 2.0 - interactive websites, social media platforms, and user-generated content. Companies like Google, Amazon, and Facebook grew from startups into global giants. The rise of smartphones in the late 2000s made the internet truly ubiquitous, with mobile internet access surpassing desktop usage by 2016.

Today, the internet faces new challenges including privacy concerns, misinformation, digital divide, and cybersecurity threats. The Internet of Things (IoT) connects billions of devices, while artificial intelligence and cloud computing continue to reshape how we use networked technology.

questions.question: ARPANET was created by the US Department of Defense during the Cold War.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'the United States Department of Defense sought to create a communication network... The result was ARPANET.'|CN: 文章指出'美国国防部试图创建一个通信网络...结果就是ARPANET。'

questions.question: When was ARPANET's first message sent?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'ARPANET sent its first message on October 29, 1969.'|CN: 文章明确指出'ARPANET在1969年10月29日发送了第一条消息。'

questions.question: ARPANET was designed to be centralized for better control.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The system was designed to be decentralized.'|CN: 文章指出'该系统被设计为去中心化的。'

questions.question: Who developed the TCP/IP protocol suite? (Two names connected by 'and')
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn developed the TCP/IP protocol suite.'|CN: 文章指出'Vint Cerf和Bob Kahn开发了TCP/IP协议套件。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: TCP/IP breaks data into _______, sends them across the network, and reassembles them at the destination.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'TCP/IP breaks data into packets, sends them across the network, and reassembles them at the destination.'|CN: 文章指出'TCP/IP将数据分解为数据包，通过网络发送，并在目的地重新组装。'

questions.question: Tim Berners-Lee invented the internet.
questions.explanation: EN: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web, not the internet itself. The internet existed before the Web.|CN: Tim Berners-Lee发明的是万维网，而非互联网本身。互联网在万维网之前就已存在。

questions.question: What was the name of the first web browser mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The first web browser, Mosaic, was released in 1993.'|CN: 文章指出'第一个网页浏览器Mosaic于1993年发布。'

questions.question: Mobile internet access surpassed desktop usage by 2016.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'mobile internet access surpassing desktop usage by 2016.'|CN: 文章指出'移动互联网接入在2016年超过了桌面使用。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Tim Berners-Lee introduced HTML, HTTP, and _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states he 'introduced HTML, HTTP, and URLs.'|CN: 文章指出他'引入了HTML、HTTP和URL。'

questions.question: What term describes the era of interactive websites and user-generated content that began in the 2000s? (One word with a number)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The 2000s brought Web 2.0 - interactive websites, social media platforms, and user-generated content.'|CN: 文章指出'2000年代带来了Web 2.0——交互式网站、社交媒体平台和用户生成内容。'

questions.question: The text mentions five challenges facing the internet today.
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions four challenges: privacy concerns, misinformation, digital divide, and cybersecurity threats.|CN: 文章提到了四个挑战：隐私问题、虚假信息、数字鸿沟和网络安全威胁。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: The Internet of Things connects billions of _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The Internet of Things (IoT) connects billions of devices.'|CN: 文章指出'物联网连接了数十亿台设备。'

questions.question: What year did TCP/IP become the standard for internet communication?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'TCP/IP... became the standard for internet communication in 1983.'|CN: 文章指出'TCP/IP...在1983年成为互联网通信的标准。'

passages.title: Ocean Plastic Pollution
passages.text:
Every year, approximately 8 million tonnes of plastic enter the ocean - equivalent to dumping a garbage truck full of plastic into the sea every minute. If current trends continue, scientists project that by 2050, there will be more plastic in the ocean than fish by weight.

Plastic pollution comes from various sources. Land-based sources account for about 80% of marine plastic, with the remainder coming from ocean-based activities such as fishing and shipping. Rivers act as major conduits, carrying plastic waste from inland areas to the sea. Research indicates that just ten river systems - eight in Asia and two in Africa - transport approximately 90% of all plastic entering the ocean.

Once in the ocean, plastic does not biodegrade. Instead, it breaks down into smaller pieces called microplastics through exposure to sunlight, wave action, and friction. These microplastics, defined as particles smaller than 5mm, are particularly problematic because they are easily ingested by marine organisms.

The impact on marine life is devastating. Sea turtles often mistake plastic bags for jellyfish. Seabirds feed plastic fragments to their chicks. Whales and dolphins become entangled in discarded fishing gear. Studies have found microplastics in over 100 marine species, including fish that humans consume.

Microplastics have also entered the human food chain. They have been detected in tap water, beer, salt, and seafood. While the health effects of ingesting microplastics are not yet fully understood, preliminary research suggests potential concerns related to chemical additives and the accumulation of toxins.

Solutions to ocean plastic pollution are being pursued at multiple levels. Many countries have banned single-use plastics. International agreements aim to reduce plastic waste. Technological innovations include biodegradable alternatives to conventional plastics, improved recycling systems, and ocean cleanup devices. Individual actions, such as reducing plastic consumption and properly disposing of waste, also contribute to the solution.

questions.question: Approximately how much plastic enters the ocean each year?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'approximately 8 million tonnes of plastic enter the ocean' every year.|CN: 文章指出每年'约800万吨塑料进入海洋。'

questions.question: By 2050, there will definitely be more plastic than fish in the ocean.
questions.explanation: EN: The text says 'scientists project' this outcome - it is a projection, not a certainty.|CN: 文章说是'科学家预测'这一结果——是预测而非确定事实。

questions.question: Land-based sources account for 80% of marine plastic pollution.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Land-based sources account for about 80% of marine plastic.'|CN: 文章指出'陆地来源约占海洋塑料的80%。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Just ten river systems transport approximately _______ of all plastic entering the ocean.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'just ten river systems... transport approximately 90% of all plastic entering the ocean.'|CN: 文章指出'仅十条河流系统...输送了进入海洋的所有塑料的约90%。'

questions.question: What term describes plastic particles smaller than 5mm in the ocean? (One word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text defines 'microplastics, defined as particles smaller than 5mm.'|CN: 文章定义了'微塑料，即小于5毫米的颗粒。'

questions.question: How many marine species have been found to contain microplastics according to the passage?
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Studies have found microplastics in over 100 marine species.'|CN: 文章指出'研究发现超过100种海洋物种体内含有微塑料。'

questions.question: The health effects of ingesting microplastics are well understood by scientists.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'the health effects of ingesting microplastics are not yet fully understood.'|CN: 文章指出'摄入微塑料对健康的影响尚未完全明确。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Plastic breaks down into smaller pieces through exposure to sunlight, wave action, and _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions 'exposure to sunlight, wave action, and friction' as factors breaking down plastic.|CN: 文章提到'暴露在阳光下、波浪作用和摩擦'是分解塑料的因素。

questions.question: What do sea turtles mistake plastic bags for? (One word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Sea turtles often mistake plastic bags for jellyfish.'|CN: 文章指出'海龟经常把塑料袋误认为是水母。'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a solution to ocean plastic pollution?
options: C. Building more dams on rivers
questions.correctAnswer: C. Building more dams on rivers
questions.explanation: EN: The text mentions banning single-use plastics, international agreements, biodegradable alternatives, improved recycling, and ocean cleanup - not dams.|CN: 文章提到了禁止一次性塑料、国际协议、可生物降解替代品、改进回收和海洋清理——但没有提到建水坝。

questions.question: Ocean-based activities contribute 20% of marine plastic pollution.
questions.explanation: EN: Since land-based sources account for 80%, the remaining 20% comes from ocean-based activities.|CN: 由于陆地来源占80%，剩余的20%来自海洋活动。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: Microplastics have been detected in tap water, beer, salt, and _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states microplastics 'have been detected in tap water, beer, salt, and seafood.'|CN: 文章指出微塑料'已在自来水、啤酒、盐和海鲜中被检测到。'

questions.question: How many of the top ten river systems that carry plastic to the ocean are in Asia? (Number only)
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'eight in Asia and two in Africa' among the ten river systems.|CN: 文章指出十条河流系统中'八条在亚洲，两条在非洲。'

passages.title: Sleep and Memory Consolidation
passages.text:
Sleep has long been recognized as essential for health, but recent research has revealed its crucial role in memory consolidation - the process by which short-term memories are transformed into long-term storage.

The sleep cycle consists of several stages, broadly categorized into REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep and non-REM sleep. Each stage plays a distinct role in memory processing. During deep non-REM sleep, the brain replays and strengthens declarative memories - facts, events, and knowledge. REM sleep, on the other hand, appears to be important for procedural memories, such as learning how to play a musical instrument or ride a bicycle.

Neuroimaging studies have shown that during sleep, the hippocampus - a brain region critical for memory formation - communicates with the neocortex, where long-term memories are stored. This dialogue involves the transfer of information from the hippocampus, which has limited storage capacity, to the neocortex, which has vast capacity. Think of it as moving files from a temporary folder to a permanent archive.

Sleep also plays a role in emotional memory processing. The brain appears to strip away the emotional charge from memories during REM sleep, allowing us to recall difficult events without the same level of distress. This may explain why people with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) often experience disrupted sleep, particularly in the REM stage.

The timing of sleep relative to learning matters. Studies show that people who sleep within a few hours of learning new information retain it significantly better than those who stay awake for extended periods. A short nap can also improve memory retention. Even a 6-minute nap has been shown to enhance recall in some studies.

Age affects the relationship between sleep and memory. Older adults typically experience less deep sleep, which may contribute to age-related memory decline. However, research suggests that improving sleep quality in older adults can enhance memory function, highlighting the importance of sleep hygiene across the lifespan.

Practical implications are clear: for students, getting a good night's sleep before an exam is more beneficial than pulling an all-nighter. For anyone learning new skills, prioritizing sleep is as important as the practice itself.

questions.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: B. How sleep helps memory consolidation
D. The effects of aging on sleep
questions.correctAnswer: B. How sleep helps memory consolidation
questions.explanation: EN: The passage focuses on the role of sleep in memory consolidation, including how different sleep stages process different types of memories.|CN: 文章主要讨论睡眠在记忆巩固中的作用，包括不同睡眠阶段如何处理不同类型的记忆。

questions.question: REM sleep is important for declarative memories.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states that non-REM sleep is for declarative memories, while REM sleep is for procedural memories.|CN: 文章指出非快速眼动睡眠负责陈述性记忆，而快速眼动睡眠负责程序性记忆。

questions.question: The hippocampus has limited storage capacity for memories.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states the hippocampus has 'limited storage capacity' compared to the neocortex.|CN: 文章指出海马体的存储容量有限。

questions.question: Complete the sentence: During sleep, the hippocampus communicates with the _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'the hippocampus... communicates with the neocortex' during sleep.|CN: 文章指出睡眠期间'海马体...与新皮层进行交流。'

questions.question: What does the brain do with emotional memories during REM sleep?
options: D. Transfers them to the hippocampus
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'The brain appears to strip away the emotional charge from memories during REM sleep.'|CN: 文章指出'大脑似乎在快速眼动睡眠期间剥离记忆的情感负荷。'

questions.question: Sleep within how many hours of learning helps retain information better? (Number + 'hours')
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'people who sleep within a few hours of learning new information retain it significantly better.'|CN: 文章指出'在学习新信息后几小时内睡觉的人能显著更好地记住信息。'

questions.question: A 6-minute nap is too short to improve memory.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Even a 6-minute nap has been shown to enhance recall in some studies.'|CN: 文章指出'一些研究表明即使是6分钟的小睡也能增强记忆回忆。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: People with PTSD often experience disrupted sleep, particularly in the _______ stage.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states people with PTSD 'often experience disrupted sleep, particularly in the REM stage.'|CN: 文章指出创伤后应激障碍患者'经常经历睡眠中断，特别是在快速眼动睡眠阶段。'

questions.question: Why might older adults experience memory decline according to the passage?
options: B. They typically experience less deep sleep
C. They stop learning new things
questions.correctAnswer: B. They typically experience less deep sleep
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'Older adults typically experience less deep sleep, which may contribute to age-related memory decline.'|CN: 文章指出'老年人通常经历较少深度睡眠，这可能导致与年龄相关的记忆衰退。'

questions.question: Improving sleep quality can enhance memory function in older adults.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'improving sleep quality in older adults can enhance memory function.'|CN: 文章指出'改善老年人的睡眠质量可以增强记忆功能。'

questions.question: Complete the sentence: For students, getting a good night's sleep before an exam is more beneficial than _______.
questions.explanation: EN: The text states 'getting a good night's sleep before an exam is more beneficial than pulling an all-nighter.'|CN: 文章指出'考试前睡个好觉比熬夜更有益。'

questions.question: What is the process called by which short-term memories become long-term? (Two-word answer)
questions.explanation: EN: The text defines 'memory consolidation - the process by which short-term memories are transformed into long-term storage.'|CN: 文章定义了'记忆巩固——短期记忆转化为长期存储的过程。'

questions.question: The passage states that sleep is not important for people learning new skills.
questions.explanation: EN: The text concludes 'prioritizing sleep is as important as the practice itself' for learning skills.|CN: 文章总结道'优先保证睡眠与练习本身同样重要。'

questions.question: What does the passage compare the transfer of memories from hippocampus to neocortex to?
options: A. Water flowing from a river to the ocean
B. Moving files from a temporary folder to a permanent archive
C. A computer backing up data to the cloud
D. Books being moved from one library to another
questions.correctAnswer: B. Moving files from a temporary folder to a permanent archive
questions.explanation: EN: The text uses the analogy 'Think of it as moving files from a temporary folder to a permanent archive.'|CN: 文章用比喻'可以把它想象成将文件从临时文件夹移动到永久存档。'
